verdict.answer_first: A leads with bottom line: "swallows every failure silently — that's the core problem"; B also starts clearly but adds setup: "classic... mechanics are correct".
verdict.decisiveness: A gives one prescription: "handle the specific expected error... If no, don't catch it"; B surveys "Better options" with 3 alternatives plus a scan CTA.
verdict.fabrication: Both use unverified examples like "out-of-memory"; B adds broader unsupported claim "most linters... will flag this".
verdict.actionability: A is easier to apply because it reduces the decision to recoverable vs not; B has useful snippets but more branches.
